questions.question_text: Under SC law, a BIC's supervisory authority over affiliated licensees can be delegated to:
questions.correct_answer: No one — BIC authority cannot be delegated
distractors: A senior associate with 5+ years experience
An office manager with a PM license
Any licensed broker within the firm
questions.explanation: The BIC role is personal and cannot be delegated to another licensee. The BIC is personally responsible for supervision, trust account maintenance, and all office operations.

questions.question_text: Which document is the BIC required to maintain under SC state law?
distractors: A quarterly financial report for LLR
questions.explanation: Section 40-57-137(B) requires the BIC to maintain a written policy manual. This is a specific SC requirement — not all states mandate a policy manual.

questions.question_text: Under the 2024 Practice Act, an associate may be affiliated with how many BICs?
distractors: As many BICs as they choose
questions.explanation: The 2024 Practice Act clarified that an associate can only have one Broker-in-Charge. The BIC is responsible for the associate's day-to-day management, trust accounts, and compliance.

questions.question_text: A BIC delegates trust account bookkeeping to an office assistant. If an accounting error occurs, who is responsible?
distractors: The office assistant who made the error
Both the BIC and assistant equally
questions.explanation: While a BIC may delegate bookkeeping tasks, the BIC retains personal responsibility for the trust account. BIC authority and accountability cannot be transferred even when tasks are delegated.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a required duty of the BIC?
questions.correct_answer: Reviewing forms used by affiliated licensees
questions.explanation: BIC duties include reviewing and approving forms, supervising trust accounts, maintaining contact with licensees, instructing on trust fund handling, and being reasonably available to the public.

questions.question_text: A PMIC's role is most similar to which other position in SC real estate?
distractors: A title attorney for lease closings
A mortgage broker for rental investors
questions.explanation: The PMIC supervises affiliated property managers, maintains PM trust accounts, and ensures compliance — similar to how a BIC supervises sales operations. SC's separate PM license category makes this a distinct role.

questions.question_text: A PMIC collects security deposits from tenants. Where must these funds be held?
distractors: In the property owner's personal account
In the PMIC's personal savings account
questions.explanation: Security deposits and advance rental deposits are trust funds and must be held in the PM trust account. The PMIC is responsible for ensuring compliance with trust account rules.

questions.question_text: A property management company handles both sales and rentals. What is the best practice for trust accounts?
distractors: One combined trust account is sufficient
Three accounts: sales, rental, and reserves
No trust account needed for rental operations
questions.explanation: Best practice is to maintain two trust accounts: one for earnest money/sales and one for rental security deposits/advance rent. This keeps sales and PM trust funds clearly separated.

questions.question_text: What additional requirement must a PMIC fulfill beyond holding a property manager license?
distractors: Hold a broker license for 2 years
Obtain a separate PMIC exam certification
questions.explanation: A PMIC must hold a PM license (30 hours + PM exam) plus complete a 7-hour PMIC class. There is no separate PMIC exam — just the additional course on top of the PM license.

questions.question_text: A PMIC manages 50 rental units across 3 property owners. What bookkeeping requirement applies?
questions.correct_answer: Maintain a separate owner's ledger for each owner
distractors: One combined ledger for all units is sufficient
Only a single bank statement is required
Separate ledgers only for owners with 10+ units
questions.explanation: The PMIC must maintain an owner's ledger showing receipts and disbursements for each owner's properties, plus a tenant's ledger for each tenant. This applies regardless of the number of units per owner.

questions.question_text: An associate receives a $3,000 earnest money check on a Thursday. By when must the associate deliver it to the BIC?
distractors: At the next weekly office meeting
questions.explanation: Under SC law, trust funds received by a licensee must be delivered to the BIC or PMIC no later than the next business day. The BIC then must deposit it within 48 hours after offer acceptance.

questions.question_text: An SC associate's advertising must include which identifying information?
questions.explanation: All advertising must clearly identify the full name of the brokerage firm. Associates cannot advertise independently without firm identification — blind ads are prohibited.

questions.question_text: A buyer makes a verbal offer through an SC associate. What must the associate do?
distractors: Present it verbally to the listing agent
Record it in the office phone log
questions.explanation: SC licensees must reduce all offers, acceptances, and changes to writing. Verbal offers should be put in written form before presentation to ensure proper documentation.

questions.question_text: An associate works for a brokerage that is part of a national franchise. What must the associate disclose?
questions.explanation: SC licensees must disclose the franchise name if applicable, in addition to the brokerage firm's full name. This ensures consumers know the business relationships involved.

questions.question_text: Under the 2024 team advertising rules (effective May 2027), a team name must include which word?
questions.explanation: The 2024 Practice Act requires team names to include the word 'team' in the format '— Team at — Brokerage.' The brokerage name must appear at least half the size and half as many times as the team name.
